The Quick Duck Coat, Heated Down Vest and FR Fleece Sweatshirt drop to grade 1: their DWR / water-repellent claims live ONLY in the description, so title-only search cannot legitimately know they are waterproof. Expect a real nDCG drop here - that drop IS the finding.

notes: 'Blue Willow Eggshell Paint with Primer' DEMOTED 2 -> 1: its title never says 'Interior' (only the description does), so a title-only engine cannot verify it is interior paint. Everything else holds, because BEHR/PPG encode color and finish directly in the title. The 11 no-'blue'-token paints ('Azure Tide', 'Cool Sky', 'Layers of Ocean'...) still cap BM25's recall. CAVEAT: the CSV has no color/hex column, so blue-family membership is inferred from the color name and BEHR/PPG code - treat those grade-1 labels as judgment, not ground truth.

notes: EASIER than the title+description version, and that is the interesting part. THREE relevant items were REMOVED because 'mirror' appears nowhere in their title: the Sonoma mirrored medicine cabinet, the Alistair 'vanity and mirror duo', and the lighted dressing-table vanity. Title-only cannot find them at all - so its recall ceiling is lower. But SIX distractors also vanished: the three VANITY LIGHTS (whose descriptions say 'above a bathroom mirror') and the three vanities whose descriptions say the mirror is 'sold separately'. Net effect: title-only should show HIGHER precision and LOWER recall than title+description. That trade-off is the whole point of running both.

keyword: something to keep my dog from escaping the yard

grading_scale.2: Title alone conveys containment - an animal dig barrier or a fence/enclosure kit.
grading_scale.1: Fence-related but only partial - a fence gate, or a privacy screen for an EXISTING fence (blocks sight, not escape).

known_distractors.why: Vinyl fence POST BASE TRIM - a cosmetic accessory

notes: HARDEST case for title-only, and the sharpest demonstration of why you concatenate. The two grade-2 items keep their grade because their TITLES do carry the concept ('Large Animal Dig Barrier', 'Fence Panel/Enclosure Kit with Gate Insert') - but the words that actually tie them to a DOG ('Secure your home gardens, PETS, chickens'; 'extends the protection of any fence') exist ONLY in the description. Meanwhile all 8 dog-themed decor distractors (beds, mats, flags, ornaments) DO have 'dog' or 'pet' in the title. So a title-only semantic engine is pulled hard toward the decor and away from the correct answers. Expect the worst score of all five queries here.
